name: comprehensive-code-review
description: This skill should be used when reviewing pull requests, performing comprehensive code review, analyzing code changes before merge, or when the user asks for thorough/ultra-critical code review. Performs EXTREMELY CRITICAL 6-pass analysis identifying runtime failures, code consistency issues, architectural problems, environment compatibility risks, and verification strategies. Posts structured review as GitHub PR comment. Use when user asks to "review PR", "review this code", "review changes", "check this PR", "analyze PR", "post review", or for Phase 3 of devflow. Supports parallel review mode with multiplier (code-review-3, code-review 6X) for consensus-based reviews. This is an ultra-critical reviewer that does not let things slip and desires only perfection.
capabilities: Posts reviews directly to GitHub PRs
Creates Mermaid diagrams for visual explanations
author: Cygnusfear
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill providing code review methodology. It does not execute arbitrary code or access external systems. The 237 static findings are all FALSE POSITIVES - the scanner flagged legitimate GitHub CLI commands (gh pr view, gh pr diff), git operations (git diff), and documentation examples as potential security risks. All external commands are hardcoded git/GitHub tooling for reading PR data and diffs - standard code review operations with no command injection risk. No malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or harmful patterns confirmed.
valueStatement: Catch bugs before they reach production with systematic 6-pass code reviews. Get actionable feedback on runtime failures, architecture issues, and verification strategies.
userTitle: Review pull requests with ultra-critical analysis
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI for PR operations
Needs git repository with PR structure
Requires PR number for GitHub posting

useCases.title: Pre-merge quality gates
useCases.description: Block merging of PRs with critical issues by requesting changes with detailed technical feedback.

useCases.title: Architecture review
useCases.description: Identify refactoring opportunities, code duplication, and architectural improvements before code reaches production.

useCases.title: Contribution validation
useCases.description: Ensure external contributions meet project standards and properly implement all requirements.

promptTemplates.title: Basic PR Review
promptTemplates.prompt: code-review Review this PR and post findings as a GitHub comment

promptTemplates.title: Parallel Review
promptTemplates.prompt: code-review-3 Use 3 parallel reviewers for critical analysis

promptTemplates.title: Local Changes
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my local changes before I create a PR

promptTemplates.title: DevFlow Phase 3
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform Phase 3 review on these changes before merging

bestPractices: Always provide the PR number when available for automatic GitHub posting
Use parallel review mode for critical changes requiring high confidence
Review local changes before creating PRs to catch issues early
Request specific fixes rather than giving percentage scores
antiPatterns: Do not approve PRs with any unaddressed issues - aim for 10/10 quality
Avoid reviewing without understanding the original issue requirements
Never skip the 100% requirement coverage verification
Do not post reviews only locally - always share on GitHub

faq.question: Is this compatible with GitHub Enterprise?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill uses standard GitHub CLI commands which work with GitHub Enterprise instances.

faq.question: What file types can it review?
faq.answer: Any text-based source code files including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, and more.

faq.question: Can it integrate with my CI/CD pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes, you can run reviews as part of your CI process and fail builds on critical findings.

faq.question: How does parallel review mode work?
faq.answer: Launches multiple independent Claude instances that analyze the same changes, then synthesizes their findings for higher confidence results.

faq.question: Does it modify my code?
faq.answer: No, this is a review-only tool. It analyzes code and provides feedback but never modifies source files.

faq.question: How is this different from automated linters?
faq.answer: Provides contextual analysis beyond syntax - architecture review, requirement verification, and strategic improvement suggestions.
